description: Use this API to replicate the working case in the form of a power flow raw data file. This API routine was first introduced in release 32.0.0. ierr = rawd_2(sid, all, status, out, ofile)
python_syntax: ierr = rawd_2(sid, all, status, out, ofile)

parameters.name: sid
parameters.description: A valid subsystem identifier. valid subsystem identifiers range from 0 to 11. subsystem sid must have been previously defined. (input; 0 by default).

parameters.name: all
parameters.description: All buses or specified subsystem flag. (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: An array of seven elements specifying options. (input).

parameters.name: ofile
parameters.description: Name of the file to which data records are to be written (when out is 0). (input; no default allowed).

all.description: process only buses in subsystem SID.
process all buses when STATUS(4) = 0; only area tie branches when STATUS(4) = 1.

status.description: include records for Type 4 buses (1 by default).
no.
yes.
include records for out-of-service branches (1 by default).
no.
yes.
include records for equipment in the subsystem (1 by default).
no.
yes.
include records for subsystem tie branches (0 by default).
no.
yes.
include load record option (honored when ALL=0) (0 by default).
for all loads at subsystem buses.
for subsystem loads at all buses.
for all loads at subsystem buses and subsystem loads at non-subsystem buses.
use bus names as bus identifiers (0 by default).
no.
yes.
Raw Data File type (0 by default).
for activity READ with IC=0.
for activity READ with IC=1.
for activity RDCH; include all data categories.
for activity RDCH; exclude the substation data category.
for activity RDCH; include only the substation data category.

out.description: direct output to a data file specified by OFILE.
direct output to the current report device.

error_codes.description: invalid SID value or subsystem SID is not defined.
prerequisite requirements for API are not met.
